text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  0 1
Research challenge

paragraphs.text: Scale, trust, and compliance collide: identity risk, resume fraud, industrial duplicates, and India offer or BGC flexibility clash with US-default rigidity.
Governance sits outside Workday: email approvals, OTP and ID friction, weak acceptance signals, and task noise push leaders to parallel tools.
Competitive scans show true gaps on native +91 SMS, WhatsApp in core UI, UIDAI Aadhaar eKYC, and Naukri-class multipost; suite, UDMF, and BGV remain differentiators.
Priority bets: government-ID-aware dedupe, hardened identity capture, bulk review with governed AI, offer or EA lifecycle, audited career-site PII edits, in-system approvals.

speaker_notes:
• Stress trust chain plus throughput as one problem.
• Cite DPDP and labour code timing as forcing functions.
• Land on partner-truth for channels and boards.

References:
• PMF analysis: research/India/thematic-analysis/2026-03-31-India-PMF-Analysis-IN-E2E-005.md

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  0 2
Research challenge

paragraphs.text: How should Workday Recruiting win India enterprise and high-volume programmes on trust, compliance, and recruiter throughput?
Objectives: validate funnel frictions, channel reality, identity and dedupe needs, offer or BGC flexibility, and governance gaps versus global suite strengths.
Methods: strategy context, PESTEL, SWOT, competitive intelligence, five internal SMEs, five customer voices (P1–P5), thematic synthesis with triangulation.
Scope: India-hosted recruiting; presales win or loss exports and ideation CSV corpora were not available for this cycle (explicit data-gap slides follow).

speaker_notes:
• Keep questions executive; avoid methodology jargon in-room.
• Note data gaps honestly; primary research still converges.

References:
• Strategy context: research/India/strategy-context-2026-03-31-IN-E2E-005.md

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  0 3
Context review

paragraphs.text: Labour Codes central commencement (21 Nov 2025) and DPDP phased rules increase documentation, consent, and audit expectations across hiring and onboarding.
IMARC sizes India ATS near USD 0.30B (2024) toward USD 0.50B by 2033 (~7.2% CAGR); economic growth supports continued enterprise ATS investment.
Buyers compare suite depth with local omnichannel stories; honest parity on SMS, WhatsApp, Aadhaar, and boards is now an RFP fixture.
Workday’s Q2 India row targets eight customers with DPDP compliance and local board reach as named pillars alongside global AI and GCC bets.

speaker_notes:
• Tie political and legal timing to sales urgency.
• Anchor growth stats to IMARC and strategy markdown.

References:
• PESTEL: research/India/pestel-analysis-India-2026-03-31-IN-E2E-005.md
• Strategy: strategy/markdown/product-priorities-q2-2026.md

paragraphs.text: HCM software market (India) cited near USD 927.5M (2024) with double-digit CAGR outlooks to early 2030s (IMARC, 2026 fetch).
WhatsApp user bases in India cited in mid-hundreds of millions; mobile-first apply and recruiter workflows are default, not optional.
Public cloud in India sized ~USD 10.9B (2024) toward ~USD 30.4B by 2029 (~22.6% CAGR, IDC narrative) supporting SaaS delivery and integrations.
GenAI in HR is widely piloted but shallow on outcomes; privacy brakes reinforce consent-first automation positioning for screening and matching.

speaker_notes:
• Use ranges cautiously; cite analyst definitions.
• Bridge cloud growth to integration and security expectations.

References:
• IMARC HCM and ATS pages; IDC India cloud PR

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  0 4
Product strategy

paragraphs.text: India scale growth: eight customers targeted in Q2 with DPDP compliance programmes and local job board reach via certified partner distribution.
AI candidate matching: HiredScore activation with explainability and human review aligns to India volume hiring when consent and oversight are credible.
Core ATS parity: bulk actions, mobile recruiter experience, BGV integrations, and Paradox scheduling reduce table-stakes objections in India cycles.
GCC market readiness remains corporate Priority 1; messaging and compliance patterns there still inform India buyer expectations on channels and auditability.

speaker_notes:
• Lead with India row first; then corporate priorities for context.
• Cross-check product-priorities-q2-2026.md.

References:
• research/India/strategy-context-2026-03-31-IN-E2E-005.md

paragraphs.text: India: medium priority; eight Q2 wins; DPDP compliance and local boards as explicit hooks in quarterly markdown.
GCC: high priority; ten Q2 wins; WhatsApp, nationalisation, Arabic, and boards blockers drive corporate OKR focus.
Japan: medium; five expansions; two-step offer and APPI compliance deepen multinational templates.
Australia: medium; three expansions; Fair Work Act and SEEK integration maintain leadership narrative.

speaker_notes:
• Table sourced from strategy context regional expansion section.

References:
• strategy/markdown/product-priorities-q2-2026.md

paragraphs.text: Suite depth: hire-to-retire coherence, security model, and global rollout patterns versus point ATS and regional HRMS bundles.
AI-powered: HiredScore and Workday AI with human-in-the-loop versus undifferentiated agentic demos from enterprise incumbents.
Compliance-first: configurable consent, retention, and purge levers mapped to DPDP expectations when paired with customer legal programmes.
Vulnerabilities: job board coverage via partners first, mobile recruiter parity gaps, and scheduling depth tied to Paradox activation.

speaker_notes:
• Align to strategy context competitive positioning bullets.

References:
• research/India/strategy-context-2026-03-31-IN-E2E-005.md

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  0 5
PESTEL

paragraphs.text: Four Labour Codes commenced centrally on 21 Nov 2025, consolidating 29 legacy statutes; state rules still evolve (Mint, Lexology commentary).
EPFO pushed UAN onboarding via UMANG with Aadhaar face authentication from 1 Aug 2025 (Indian Express, EPFO circulars).
Code on Social Security 2020 framing plus e-Shram registration narratives expand formal workforce coverage (PIB-style reporting).
Centre-state alignment on wages and OSH rules remains uneven; enterprises expect configurable hiring documentation and audit trails.

text.text: Workday should ship India labour-code-aware hiring templates, contract-type configurability, and clear boundaries between recruiting data and benefits enrolment integrations.

speaker_notes:
• Present factor anchors; keep implication visible.

References:
• research/India/pestel-analysis-India-2026-03-31-IN-E2E-005.md

paragraphs.text: IMF and OECD commentary cluster near 6–7%+ real GDP growth for FY26–FY27 (Indian press summaries, Jan 2026).
IMARC cites India HCM at USD 927.5M (2024) toward USD 2,861.9M by 2033 at 12.35% CAGR (2025–2033).
India ATS sized USD 0.30B (2024) toward USD 0.50B by 2033 at ~7.2% CAGR; drivers include AI, cloud, remote hiring, and labour compliance.
Naukri JobSpeak shows resilient white-collar hiring with strong non-IT pockets and AI role growth in 2025 commentary.

text.text: Prioritise cloud-native, compliance-aware differentiators that defend suite TCO against point ATS vendors while capturing ATS growth inside the HCM envelope.

speaker_notes:
• Present factor anchors; keep implication visible.

References:
• research/India/pestel-analysis-India-2026-03-31-IN-E2E-005.md

paragraphs.text: India is WhatsApp’s largest national market; third-party summaries cite ~535–550M+ users and very high smartphone-user penetration (Findly, 2025–2026).
Household surveys indicate ~85.5% of households had at least one smartphone in early 2025 (NSO-style survey via public summaries).
Internet penetration narratives cite ~70% in DataReportal 2026 India reports alongside ~1.03B users (methodology-sensitive).
Enterprise hiring liquidity stays metro-heavy (Bengaluru, Pune, Gujarat industrial pockets per Naukri commentary) with English dominant in MNC recruiting.

text.text: Assume mobile apply and recruiter mobility by default; integrate partner-led WhatsApp with DPDP-aligned consent, retention, and logging on every touchpoint.

speaker_notes:
• Present factor anchors; keep implication visible.

References:
• research/India/pestel-analysis-India-2026-03-31-IN-E2E-005.md

paragraphs.text: IDC sizes India public cloud services at USD 10.9B (2024) toward USD 30.4B (2029) at 22.6% CAGR; SaaS and AI workloads are catalysts.
BCG-style India press summaries state >90% of companies pilot GenAI in HR but only ~38% see strong relevance; ~51% cite privacy concerns.
MeitY India AI Governance Guidelines launched 5 Nov 2025 under IndiaAI Mission (trust, fairness, accountability, safety principles).
CERT-In expectations on logging and incident reporting intersect candidate databases; dual duties with DPDP breach processes matter for SaaS vendors.

text.text: Ship India-ready AI with privacy-by-design defaults, tenant human review for high-impact matching, strong audit logs, and APIs suited to cloud-forward procurement.

speaker_notes:
• Present factor anchors; keep implication visible.

References:
• research/India/pestel-analysis-India-2026-03-31-IN-E2E-005.md

paragraphs.text: SEBI BRSR and BRSR Core evolution increases structured workforce and diversity disclosures for top listed entities (ESG legal commentary, 2025).
Renewable-energy hiring press cites high-teens percentage hiring growth expectations for FY25 with skill-gap and attrition risks (IANS-style summaries).
No single Indian statute mandates carbon-per-hire in ATS; environmental signal is indirect via customer sustainability reporting and sector hiring demand.
DATA GAP: limited recruiting-specific environmental regulation; still surface analytics that support customer ESG narratives without over-rotating the core ATS roadmap.

text.text: Expose diversity and funnel metrics that reconcile to HCM analytics for BRSR consumers; support skills metadata for green roles without carbon-accounting scope creep.

speaker_notes:
• Present factor anchors; keep implication visible.

References:
• research/India/pestel-analysis-India-2026-03-31-IN-E2E-005.md

paragraphs.text: DPDP Act 2023 and 2025 Rules phase in through 2026–2027 with DPBI processes; consent must be specific, informed, and withdrawable (LiveLaw, CADP summaries).
IT Act Section 43A and SPDI Rules 2011 remain operational for sensitive personal data and reasonable security practices (WIPO Lex text).
Four Labour Codes (Nov 2025) raise documentation and industrial-relations expectations; background checks need standalone consent beyond offer boilerplate (practitioner themes).
GDPR Article 22 and EU AI Act Annex III still bind EU-affected processing; high-risk recruitment AI requires human oversight and technical documentation.

text.text: Implement granular consent and notice UX, retention and erasure workflows, subprocessors governance, and AI logging that satisfies India diligence and EU deployer duties where applicable.

speaker_notes:
• Present factor anchors; keep implication visible.

References:
• research/India/pestel-analysis-India-2026-03-31-IN-E2E-005.md

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  0 6
Competitive landscape

rows: INR packaging; multipost and SMS narratives; SpringVerify BGV guides; mid-market velocity
Narrower global enterprise depth vs suite vendors
Strong for high-volume India SMB and mid-market
AI and Microsoft fabric stories; PDP landing pages; regional HRMS bundle
Enterprise security and global template depth varies
Competes on time-to-value in India enterprise
Marketplace WhatsApp and Zia mobile; TCO story; verification apps ecosystem
Enterprise RFP depth vs global HCM suite
Sets expectations on marketplace ID flows
Omnichannel and India payroll adjacency; Zwayam Amplify posting narratives
Local board reach cited in marketing

speaker_notes:
• Ground claims in in-competitive-matrix.md v1.8.

References:
• research/competitive/in/in-competitive-scan-2026-03-31-IN-E2E-005.md

rows: SmartRecruiters embedded narrative; enterprise incumbency; India press Mar 2026
Slower innovation perception vs best-of-breed ATS in some cycles
Agentic applications and Recruiting Booster narratives; global ERP footprint
Frontline AI and omnichannel flows; high-volume story Mar 2026
Raises SMS and web conversational bar
Paid Naukri integration marketing; SMB ease of use

speaker_notes:
• Align to scan headlines; avoid unsourced claims.

References:
• research/competitive/matrices/in-competitive-matrix.md

rows: Suite hire-to-retire coherence for MNC India; native BGV business process and connectors; UDMF duplicate management; DPDP-style configurable consent, retention, and purge; bulk grid and baseline matching
True gaps: native +91 SMS, native WhatsApp in core Recruiting UI, native UIDAI Aadhaar eKYC, OOTB Naukri-class multipost; mobile recruiter partial parity; Paradox-grade scheduling without partners
Q2 India eight-customer row with DPDP and boards; AI matching activation with human-in-the-loop; Labour Code Nov 2025 documentation demand; partner-led channel closure; BRSR metrics consumers
Darwinbox, Keka, Zoho omnichannel bundling; SAP and Oracle AI press; DPDP and CERT-In dual breach optics; RFP risk if Aadhaar or SMS paths are overclaimed; high-volume churn if scale pain persists

speaker_notes:
• Single four-quadrant SWOT table; semicolon-separated cells.

References:
• research/India/swot-analysis-India-2026-03-31-IN-E2E-005.md

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  0 7
Win / Loss

paragraphs.text: Presales win and loss gap exports were not run for this research cycle; no severity-weighted CRM extract is available to chart.
Roadmap prioritisation therefore leans on customer interviews, internal SME perspectives, competitive matrix true gaps, and strategy context.
When exports resume, regenerate standard gap theme, chart, and proxy slides to align sales loss reasons with product investment cases.

speaker_notes:
• Explain absence of presales gap export for this mission.
• Point to interviews and competitive intelligence for gap identification.

References:
• Mission scope: no presales gap-analysis export ingested

paragraphs.text: Top-N gap theme tables require structured opportunity fields and consistent region tagging in presales exports.
Until hygiene is restored, treat competitive scan severity labels and customer quotes as primary gap evidence, not win-loss frequency counts.
Product teams should agree a single India gap taxonomy with Sales Ops before rebuilding charts.

speaker_notes:
• Explain absence of presales gap export for this mission.
• Point to interviews and competitive intelligence for gap identification.

References:
• Mission scope: no presales gap-analysis export ingested

paragraphs.text: Horizontal bar charts of gap themes are omitted because no numeric frequency series exists for this mission.
Use competitive matrix Native, Workaround, True Gap columns plus TP India scale anecdotes to illustrate impact qualitatively.
Revisit chart slide after next presales export refresh.

speaker_notes:
• Explain absence of presales gap export for this mission.
• Point to interviews and competitive intelligence for gap identification.

References:
• Mission scope: no presales gap-analysis export ingested

paragraphs.text: Customer evidence: P1–P5 at Teleperformance India on approvals, IDs, duplicates, offers, and dashboards.
Competitive evidence: India competitive scan and matrix v1.8 with validated true-gap callouts on SMS, WhatsApp, Aadhaar, and boards.
Internal evidence: five Workday SMEs on fraud scale, offer flexibility, BGC orchestration, and WhatsApp behaviour.

speaker_notes:
• Explain absence of presales gap export for this mission.
• Point to interviews and competitive intelligence for gap identification.

References:
• Mission scope: no presales gap-analysis export ingested

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  0 9
Primary research

paragraphs.text: Five Workday SMEs: product leadership, field readiness, global services, India enterprise architecture, and Accenture strategic customer engagement.
Multi-customer visibility spans Lowe’s high-volume India paths, Genpact talent supply chain, Accenture-scale fraud and duplicates, and major partner programmes.
Timing: January 2025 through November 2024 notes depending on SME; materials support triangulation before customer readout.
Purpose: stress-test India scale, Know Your Candidate, offer or BGC flexibility, and channel behaviour hypotheses.
Limitation: internal lens supplements but does not replace P1–P5 customer voices in the following section.

speaker_notes:
• Name SMEs on next slide; customers stay P1–P5.

References:
• research/India/105-sme-research-findings.md

rows: India FY27 focus; KYC and fraud at scale
Lowe’s India volume; offers and BGC

speaker_notes:
• Cite internal transcript paths in appendix verbally.

References:
• research/India/internal-sme-transcripts/

paragraphs.text: Resume and candidate fraud framed like banking KYC; order-of-magnitude ~100k resumes per month called out.
Accenture named as a heavy segment illustrating India scale risk.
BrightHire partnership discussed for interview evidence supporting skills consistency.
India product gaps and local address rollout flagged for FY27 planning conversations.

speaker_notes:
• Tie SME claims to customer slides where possible.

References:
• 105-sme-research-findings.md

paragraphs.text: Compensation disclosure is complex on every India-scoped project: tables, calcs, often two candidate documents.
Start-date and offer changes after milestones appear more often than US templates expect.
India populations may progress before all BGC results return; customers need reinitiate and loop-back UX.
Review-document sprawl across offer and BGC business processes hurts candidate experience.

speaker_notes:
• Tie SME claims to customer slides where possible.

References:
• 105-sme-research-findings.md

paragraphs.text: Candidate attachment hub requested for non-standard documents beyond rigid templates.
ID proof before interview matters for fraud cases on a major partner programme (~7 lakh people cited).
WhatsApp preferred over SMS for back-and-forth and link sharing at India penetration.
GDPR-style marketing opt-in starves reach; clients ask about region-configurable opt-out where legal.

speaker_notes:
• Tie SME claims to customer slides where possible.

References:
• 105-sme-research-findings.md

paragraphs.text: Genpact-style programmes run 100–200 reqs with too many clicks; mass offers and approvals are needed.
Worksheets not used for some mass flows; opportunity to productise patterns.
Impersonation risk: applicant not same as interview attendee; facial recognition raised as example only.
Career site edits to name, address, and phone carry weak audit trails and BGC risk.

speaker_notes:
• Tie SME claims to customer slides where possible.

References:
• 105-sme-research-findings.md

paragraphs.text: ~200k duplicate applications cited; auto-merge limited to two records drives manual merges.
Do Not Hire auto-disposition requested against ex-employee and similar lists at India scale.
High competition drives application fraud; AI-assisted pattern detection could return time to hiring managers.
Interview-stage ID validation aligns with Lodola impersonation theme.

speaker_notes:
• Tie SME claims to customer slides where possible.

References:
• 105-sme-research-findings.md

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  1 0
Primary research

paragraphs.text: Five senior recruiting leaders and hiring managers from Teleperformance India covering specialist, leadership, and high-volume agent programmes.
Organisations operate India-scale hiring with agency networks, confidential leadership pipelines, and multi-site peaks up to thousands of hires per week.
Research conducted December 2025; semi-structured interviews on workflows, compliance, and Workday friction points.
Transcripts analysed for themes; findings triangulated with internal SMEs, competitive intelligence, and strategy context.
Participants anonymised as P1–P5 with company retained for enterprise credibility.
Limitation: single-employer deep dive; patterns require validation with additional India BPO and IT tenants.

speaker_notes:
• Keep methodology in notes; slide stays executive.

References:
• research/India/105-user-research-findings.md

• Map P IDs consistently across deck.

References:
• TP India transcripts Dec 2025

paragraphs.text: 'Sourcing, interviews, resumes, offers, and follow-up' anchor daily work for a small specialist pod.
Splits time between hands-on recruiting and team goals, attendance, and performance routines.
When leading a specialist pod, I want a single system of record for hiring steps and team admin, so I reduce parallel status chasing.

speaker_notes:
• Emphasise quotes that resonate with India scale.

References:
• P1 transcript TP India Dec 2025

paragraphs.text: 'Approvals live on email then I raise the job requisition in Workday' describes dual-system latency.
~60-person team; managers send mapped position codes via email before reqs appear in Workday.
When opening approved headcount, I want managers to raise accurate reqs with correct org and finance tags, so I avoid rework before candidates apply.

speaker_notes:
• Emphasise quotes that resonate with India scale.

References:
• P2 transcript TP India Dec 2025

paragraphs.text: "No useful Workday reports… Thrive and Excel carry progress views" summarises leadership pipeline visibility gap.
"~16–17% drop before offer accept" ties process weight to join-rate risk.
"Candidates say they do not receive OTP" highlights government ID friction before offer release.
"~700 applications… open each one separately" shows bulk review gap on high-volume reqs.
When closing leadership hires under India compliance, I want mandatory ID capture and in-system approvals, so I protect join rates and audit trails.

speaker_notes:
• Emphasise quotes that resonate with India scale.

References:
• P3 transcript TP India Dec 2025

paragraphs.text: '300-400 agencies' with manual per-upload approval consumes full days of recruiter time.
Duplicate source attribution errors drive fee disputes; first-source logic from legacy ATS is missed.
'Regenerate offer cannot fix compensation after accept' cited with hundreds of correction cases at go-live.
When running thousands of agency applications, I want government-ID-aware dedupe and fair source rules, so I remove click-approval roles and pay agencies correctly.

speaker_notes:
• Emphasise quotes that resonate with India scale.

References:
• P4 transcript TP India Dec 2025

paragraphs.text: "Saturday maintenance… India six-day week" ties platform windows to revenue-impacting downtime.
"Duplication… validate on Aadhaar number" states need beyond name, phone, and email matching.
"100–150 offers per day" with limited regenerate and no mass offer actions drives manual legal letters.
When hiring thousands weekly across sites, I want resilient onboarding IDs and scalable offer amendments, so peaks do not cost revenue or compliance risk.

speaker_notes:
• Emphasise quotes that resonate with India scale.

References:
• P5 transcript TP India Dec 2025

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  1 1
Thematic analysis

paragraphs.text: Insight: P5 wants Aadhaar-class dedupe; SMEs cite impersonation and ~100k resumes per month fraud context.
Impact: Reduces fee disputes, BGC reopen risk, and executive distrust in India programmes.
Product: Roadmap: government-ID-aware UDMF, interview-stage assurance, legal minimisation on Aadhaar claims.
Insight: P4 cites full-day vendor approvals; Phillips cites ~200k duplicates and two-record merge limits.
Impact: Manual scale drives shadow processes and agency conflict churn.
Product: Roadmap: bulk-safe vendor rules, multi-way merge policy, DNH automation, mass actions.

speaker_notes:
• Two themes per slide for density control.

References:
• 2026-03-31 India PMF Analysis IN-E2E-005

paragraphs.text: Insight: Fabiola and customers cite two-document offers, post-hire changes, and regenerate limits.
Impact: Join-rate loss when process is onerous; hundreds of compensation corrections cited.
Product: Roadmap: controlled regenerate, batch cohort amend, BGC reinitiate UX, richer pre-vendor payload.
Insight: P3 and P5 describe approvals outside Workday; screenshot audits on acceptance.
Impact: Weak in-product visibility invites Excel and Thrive workarounds.
Product: Roadmap: stage-wise approvals with attachments, role-scoped dashboards, cleaner notifications.

speaker_notes:
• Two themes per slide for density control.

References:
• 2026-03-31 India PMF Analysis IN-E2E-005

paragraphs.text: Insight: WhatsApp default socially; matrix lists native WhatsApp and +91 SMS as true gaps.
Impact: Santosh notes opt-in starves marketing reach at India volumes.
Product: Roadmap: partner reference architectures with consent, logging, opt-out; avoid false native claims.
Insight: P3 cannot parse from major boards; optional resume inflates review cost.
Impact: Bernie and Phillips emphasise fraud detection need at extreme volumes.
Product: Roadmap: bulk disposition, mandatory resume option, HiredScore activation with human oversight.

speaker_notes:
• Two themes per slide for density control.

References:
• 2026-03-31 India PMF Analysis IN-E2E-005

• Walk row by row; cite convergence verbally.

References:
• PMF triangulation table IN-E2E-005

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  1 2
Full funnel

rows: Limited native multipost to Naukri-class boards
Validate board lists per deal; honest RFP tables
Government ID and OTP friction before offer
P3 OTP failures; policy vs UX mismatch
No bulk candidate review; optional resume
Bulk grid disposition; mandatory resume option
Offer lifecycle: notify, regenerate, batch amend
In-system approvals with attachments and audit

speaker_notes:
• Severity is directional; validate with Deployment Agent in sales cycles.

References:
• PMF recommendations and TP India interviews

text_boxes.text:
S E C T I O N  1 3
Roadmap

paragraphs.text: Extend UDMF and source-of-hire for India-legal PAN, permitted Aadhaar use cases, and UAN with minimisation and legal sign-off.
Add first-source wins within cooling-off and bulk-safe vendor approval rules that preserve rehire and compliance constraints.
RICE ~2267 directional from PMF table; reach ~8000 recruiters quarterly proxy.

speaker_notes:
• Tie each recommendation to metrics named in PMF success metric section.

References:
• 2026-03-31 India PMF Analysis IN-E2E-005

0.text: Recommendation 2: Identity Capture and OTP Paths

paragraphs.text: Align mandatory government ID steps with UX; persist fields; surface OTP failure paths for candidates and recruiters.
Pair notices and consent flows with DPDP programme guidance from customer legal teams.
RICE ~4200 directional; highest score in PMF table.

speaker_notes:
• Tie each recommendation to metrics named in PMF success metric section.

References:
• 2026-03-31 India PMF Analysis IN-E2E-005

paragraphs.text: Bulk shortlist and disposition for high-volume reqs; optional mandatory resume; activate HiredScore with explainability defaults.
Align to fraud detection roadmap themes and EU AI Act human oversight narrative for enterprise buyers.
RICE ~2362 directional.

speaker_notes:
• Tie each recommendation to metrics named in PMF success metric section.

References:
• 2026-03-31 India PMF Analysis IN-E2E-005

0.text: Recommendation 4: Career Site PII Audit Trail

paragraphs.text: Audited edits for name, address, and phone on external career experiences with recruiter-visible risk flags tied to BGC.
Reduce impersonation and mismatch reopen loops called out by Lodola and trust themes.
RICE ~2340 directional.

speaker_notes:
• Tie each recommendation to metrics named in PMF success metric section.

References:
• 2026-03-31 India PMF Analysis IN-E2E-005

paragraphs.text: First-party approval workflows for position and compensation with attachments and history; cut email parallel system.
Supported unanimously by P1–P5 narratives on governance gaps.
RICE ~2109 directional.

speaker_notes:
• Tie each recommendation to metrics named in PMF success metric section.

References:
• 2026-03-31 India PMF Analysis IN-E2E-005

• Full sixteen-row table lives in PMF markdown; top ten shown for legibility.

References:
• Research handoff table in PMF analysis
